summary: This notebook implements a hybrid inference pipeline for RNA 3D structure prediction that combines template-based modeling, a generative diffusion model, and a de-novo fallback. It first aligns query sequences against a training pool to adapt known coordinates, then routes targets with insufficient templates to Protenix, handling long sequences via overlapping chunks and Kabsch-aligned stitching. Remaining prediction slots are filled with an idealized A-form helix fallback. Readers should learn how to combine structural alignment, external generative models, and geometric constraints to produce robust, multi-sample submissions when template availability is uneven.
competition_flow: Loads test sequences and training structures, predicts 3D atomic coordinates using a hybrid TBM + Protenix + de-novo pipeline, and exports a formatted submission CSV with multiple structural samples per target.
data_reading: pandas.read_csv for test, train, and validation sequence/label CSVs
Custom parsing for stoichiometry and chain segments
JSON serialization for Protenix input formatting
data_processing: Sequence chunking with configurable overlap for long targets
C1' atom coordinate extraction via masking and fallback heuristics
Kabsch alignment for chunk stitching and weighted linear blending of overlapping regions
Coordinate clipping and application of geometric constraints (bond lengths, self-avoidance, Laplacian smoothing)
features_engineering: Stoichiometry parsing and chain segment mapping
Domain-derived geometric constraints (5.95Å backbone bonds, 10.2Å i-i+2 distances, Laplacian smoothing, self-avoidance penalties)
ensembling: Concatenates TBM, Protenix, and de-novo predictions to fill up to 5 samples per target without weighted averaging or post-processing.
insights: Hybrid pipelines effectively mitigate template scarcity by combining alignment-based adaptation with generative modeling.
Chunking with Kabsch alignment enables processing sequences beyond model token limits while preserving geometric continuity.
Geometric constraints significantly improve the physical plausibility of predicted coordinates.
critical_findings: Collapsed coordinate detection is explicitly warned against to prevent invalid submissions.
Sequences falling below configurable similarity/identity thresholds are skipped to avoid poor adaptations.
Long sequences require careful overlap handling to prevent stitching artifacts.
